<sentence>These filaments, or rays, can be vibrantly colored in shades of purple, white, pink, or red, often with striking bands of color.</sentence>
<sentence>At the very heart of the flower are the reproductive structures, forming a prominent central hub.</sentence>
<sentence>These include five greenish or yellowish stamens and three stigmas, which stand erect and are often tipped with a bulbous end.</sentence>
<sentence>Surrounding the corona and reproductive parts are the five large, often waxy, petals, which typically lie flat.</sentence>
<sentence>Behind the petals, there are five sepals, which are usually similar in color to the petals but may be slightly more pointed.</sentence>
<sentence>The overall form is complex and layered, creating a sense of depth and visual intrigue.</sentence>
<sentence>Many varieties of passion flowers also possess a sweet, sometimes strong, fragrance.</sentence>
<sentence>The unique and elaborate structure of the passion flower has led to its name, symbolizing elements of the Christian Passion story.</sentence>
